article.p: Error Boundaries catch runtime errors in your component tree and render a fallback UI instead of crashing the entire app.

h2.style: font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--doc-heading-color); margin-top: 32px; margin-bottom: 16px;

article.p: Wrap your app with ErrorBoundary.wrap() before mounting:

pre.style: padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; overflow-x: auto; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px;
pre.code:
import { ErrorBoundary } from 'metaowl' import AppComponent from './App.js'
const errorBoundary = ErrorBoundary.wrap(AppComponent) errorBoundary.mount(document.body)

h2.style: font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--doc-heading-color); margin-top: 32px; margin-bottom: 16px;

article.p: Register a global handler to log or report errors:

pre.style: padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; overflow-x: auto; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px;

h2.style: font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--doc-heading-color); margin-top: 32px; margin-bottom: 16px;

article.p: Create a custom error page at src/pages/error.js to control the fallback UI:

pre.style: padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; overflow-x: auto; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px;
padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; overflow-x: auto; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px;
pre.code: Error

h2.style: font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--doc-heading-color); margin-top: 32px; margin-bottom: 16px;

article.p: The context object passed to onError contains the following fields:

tr.td: URL path where the error occurred
Name of the component that threw
Unix timestamp of when the error occurred
